books.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.description: The first collection of short stories featuring the brilliant detective Sherlock Holmes and his faithful companion Dr. Watson. Twelve cases showcase Holmes's unparalleled deductive reasoning, from A Scandal in Bohemia to The Adventure of the Copper Beeches.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: The witty Elizabeth Bennet navigates love, social class, and family pressures in Regency England as she encounters the proud Mr. Darcy. Austen's most beloved novel offers a brilliant satire of manners and a timeless romance that has captivated readers for over two centuries.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.description: Victor Frankenstein, a young scientist, creates a sapient creature through a forbidden experiment. When he abandons his creation, the monster seeks revenge. Shelley's groundbreaking novel explores themes of ambition, responsibility, and what it means to be human.

books.title: Moby-Dick; or, The Whale
books.author: Herman Melville
books.description: Sailor Ishmael joins the obsessed Captain Ahab on the whaling ship Pequod. Ahab's monomaniacal quest to hunt the great white whale Moby Dick drives the crew toward catastrophe. Melville's epic is a profound meditation on obsession, fate, and the indifferent power of nature.

books.title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
books.author: Mark Twain
books.description: Huck Finn escapes his abusive father and journeys down the Mississippi River with Jim, an escaped slave. Twain's masterpiece uses Huck's innocent perspective to deliver a searing satire of racism and hypocrisy in the antebellum American South.

books.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
books.author: Lewis Carroll
books.description: Young Alice falls down a rabbit hole into a fantastical world populated by peculiar creatures and absurd situations. Carroll's imaginative classic, filled with wordplay and logic puzzles, has enchanted readers of all ages for over 150 years.

books.title: Dracula
books.author: Bram Stoker
books.description: Told through diary entries, letters, and newspaper clippings, Stoker's gothic masterpiece follows solicitor Jonathan Harker's terrifying encounter with Count Dracula in Transylvania and the subsequent battle against the vampire in England led by the enigmatic Professor Van Helsing.

books.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.description: Dorian Gray, a beautiful young man in Victorian London, wishes that his portrait would age in his place while he remains forever young. As he sinks into hedonism and corruption, the portrait bears the marks of his sins. Wilde's only novel is a dazzling exploration of aestheticism and moral decay.

books.title: A Tale of Two Cities
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.description: Set against the backdrop of the French Revolution, Dickens weaves the fates of characters in London and Paris. Sidney Carton's final act of selfless love gives this sweeping historical novel its unforgettable conclusion and its themes of resurrection and sacrifice.

books.title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
books.author: Mark Twain
books.description: Young Tom Sawyer and his friends experience adventures in the fictional town of St. Petersburg, Missouri — from witnessing a murder in a graveyard to getting lost in a cave. Twain's beloved novel captures the mischievous spirit of boyhood along the Mississippi River.

books.title: The Land of Mist
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.description: Professor Challenger, the irascible scientist from The Lost World, is drawn into the world of Spiritualism alongside journalist Edward Malone and Challenger's daughter Enid. Through séances, mediums, and psychic phenomena, Doyle weaves a tale that blends adventure with his passionate advocacy for the Spiritualist movement.
